passages.title: Cloze Test 17-21
passages.text: The prevalence of obesity has reached epidemic proportions in many developed countries over the last few decades.

passages.title: Cloze Test 22-26
passages.text:
Professor: Your thesis proposal on behavioral economics is quite interesting. 
Student: Thank you, Professor. I'm particularly interested in consumer irrationality. 
Professor: _____
Student: I plan to conduct a series of surveys and controlled experiments.

questions.question: The rapid _____ of urban areas often leads to the degradation of local ecosystems and the loss of biodiversity.

options.A: proliferation
options.B: diminution
options.C: stagnation
options.D: recession
options.E: alleviation

questions.question: Advances in biotechnology have enabled scientists to _____ genetic sequences with unprecedented precision.

options.A: manipulate
options.B: obstruct
options.C: undermine
options.D: neglect
options.E: deteriorate

questions.question: The researcher provided a _____ account of the socio-economic factors that contributed to the industrial revolution.

options.A: comprehensive
options.B: superficial
options.C: negligible
options.D: redundant
options.E: vague

questions.question: Although the initial results of the clinical trial were promising, _____.

options.A: the medication was approved by the regulatory board immediately
options.B: the researchers decided to expand the scope of the study
options.C: subsequent phases failed to demonstrate long-term efficacy
options.D: the patient population showed significant improvement
options.E: it led to a breakthrough in the treatment of chronic pain

questions.question: Since the implementation of stricter environmental regulations, _____.

options.A: many industrial plants have reduced their carbon emissions significantly
options.B: the local economy has suffered from a lack of investment
options.C: global temperatures have remained completely stable
options.D: fossil fuels have become the primary source of energy again
options.E: the government has decided to abolish the ministry of environment

questions.question: _____ unless there is a global consensus on how to manage transboundary water resources.

options.A: Water scarcity will continue to pose a threat to regional stability
options.B: The agricultural sector will flourish in arid regions
options.C: International cooperation is no longer a necessity for peace
options.D: Domestic policies are sufficient to solve the water crisis
options.E: Hydropower will replace all other forms of energy production

questions.question: Deep-sea exploration is a challenging endeavor _____ due to the extreme pressure and darkness of the ocean floor.

options.A: which requires highly specialized equipment and expertise
options.B: it has become a popular hobby for amateur divers
options.C: where the sunlight reaches the seabed throughout the year
options.D: despite the lack of any significant biological life there
options.E: as it is cheaper than space exploration

questions.question: (Cloze Test 1/5) Throughout history, the Silk Road served as a vital (1) _____ for cultural and economic exchange between the East and the West.

options.A: conduit
options.B: barrier
options.C: vacuum
options.D: obstacle
options.E: deterrent

questions.question: (Cloze Test 2/5) It was not merely a single path but a network of trade routes (2) _____ various regions.

options.A: connecting
options.B: isolating
options.C: dividing
options.D: ignoring
options.E: restricting

questions.question: (Cloze Test 3/5) Merchants carried not only goods (3) _____ also ideas, technologies, and religions.

options.A: but
options.B: yet
options.C: either
options.D: so
options.E: nor

questions.question: (Cloze Test 4/5) This interaction (4) _____ the development of civilizations along the route.

options.A: influenced
options.B: hindered
options.C: prevented
options.D: stopped
options.E: ignored

questions.question: (Cloze Test 5/5) (5) _____, the decline of the Silk Road began with the rise of maritime trade.

options.A: However
options.B: Moreover
options.C: Furthermore
options.D: Therefore
options.E: Similarly

questions.question: Many historians argue that the fall of the Roman Empire was not an abrupt event but a gradual process of decline.

options.A: Birçok tarihçi Roma İmparatorluğu nun çöküşünün ani bir olay değil, kademeli bir düşüş süreci olduğunu savunmaktadır.
options.B: Tarihçilerin çoğu Roma İmparatorluğu nun çöküşünü ani bir olaydan ziyade yavaş bir süreç olarak görür.
options.C: Roma İmparatorluğu nun çöküşü, pek çok tarihçiye göre ani olmaktan ziyade aşamalı bir süreçti.
options.D: Roma İmparatorluğu nun çöküşünün yavaş bir süreç olduğunu iddia eden tarihçiler azınlıktadır.
options.E: Ani bir olay olmayan Roma İmparatorluğu nun çöküşü, tarihçilerce kademeli bir süreç olarak tanımlanır.

questions.question: Teknolojik gelişmeler, iş gücü piyasasının yapısını kökten değiştirmiş ve yeni becerilere olan talebi artırmıştır.

options.A: Technological advancements have radically changed the structure of the labor market and increased the demand for new skills.
options.B: The structure of the labor market has been changed by technology, creating a demand for various skills.
options.C: New skills are demanded because technological advancements changed the labor market radically.
options.D: Technology has changed the labor market so much that new skills are now required by employers.
options.E: With the change in the labor market structure, technology has increased the need for new skills.

questions.question: Despite being one of the world's most arid regions, the Atacama Desert is home to a variety of unique plant species.

options.A: Dünyanın en kurak bölgelerinden biri olmasına rağmen, Atacama Çölü çeşitli benzersiz bitki türlerine ev sahipliği yapmaktadır.
options.B: Atacama Çölü dünyanın en kurak yeridir ve orada çok az benzersiz bitki yetişir.
options.C: Dünyanın en kurak bölgeleri arasında olan Atacama Çölü, nadir bitki türlerini barındırır.
options.D: Eşsiz bitki türleri, dünyanın en kurak bölgesi olan Atacama Çölü nde hayatta kalabilir.
options.E: Atacama Çölü nün kuraklığına rağmen orada sadece birkaç bitki türü bulunmaktadır.

questions.question: Yapay zeka etiği üzerine yapılan tartışmalar, teknolojinin insan hakları üzerindeki etkisine odaklanmaktadır.

options.A: Discussions on artificial intelligence ethics focus on the impact of technology on human rights.
options.B: The impact of technology on human rights is the main focus of AI ethics.
options.C: Ethical AI discussions are focusing on how technology changes human rights.
options.D: Human rights are the core of discussions regarding the ethics of artificial intelligence.
options.E: Focusing on human rights, discussions on AI ethics are becoming more important.

questions.question: Boşluk 17 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: In many developed nations, obesity has become as widespread as an epidemic during recent decades.
options.B: Developed countries have been trying to fight the obesity epidemic for many years now.
options.C: Obesity is only a major health problem in developed countries according to recent data.
options.D: The last few decades saw a decrease in obesity rates in developed nations.
options.E: Developed countries are responsible for the global obesity epidemic of the last few years.

questions.question: Boşluk 18 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Protests broke out in the capital city immediately after the government declared the new tax policy.
options.B: The government announced the new tax policy because of the protests in the capital city.
options.C: The tax policy was announced by the government despite the ongoing protests in the capital.
options.D: As soon as the protests started, the government decided to announce a new tax policy.
options.E: The capital city remained calm until the government finalized the new tax policy.

questions.question: Boşluk 19 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The development of antibiotics is one of the most influential scientific discoveries in modern medicine.
options.B: Modern medicine would not exist without the discovery and development of antibiotics.
options.C: Antibiotics are the only scientific discovery that changed the course of modern medicine.
options.D: The impact of antibiotics on modern medicine is often overestimated by scientists.
options.E: Many scientific discoveries are just as important as antibiotics in the field of medicine.

questions.question: Boşluk 20 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Many people think that moving to renewable energy is necessary to reduce climate change's impacts.
options.B: The effects of climate change can only be stopped if we use renewable energy exclusively.
options.C: Climate change mitigation depends entirely on whether we transition to renewable energy or not.
options.D: Transitioning to renewable energy is believed to be the only way to reverse climate change.
options.E: Most scientists agree that renewable energy is the main cause of climate change mitigation.

questions.question: Boşluk 21 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: I believe most companies will eventually return to full-time office work.
options.B: It depends on the industry, but many firms are adopting hybrid models.
options.C: Remote work is far less productive than being in a physical office space.
options.D: I haven't really given much thought to the future of office work.
options.E: Most employees prefer working from home because it saves time on commuting.

questions.question: Boşluk 22 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Have you decided on the methodology you will use for your research?
options.B: Consumer irrationality is a topic that has been studied extensively already.
options.C: Economics is a field that requires a lot of mathematical knowledge.
options.D: Do you think consumers are always aware of their buying habits?
options.E: When do you expect to complete the final draft of your thesis?

questions.question: Boşluk 23 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: No, it's just the headaches, but they are quite severe at times.
options.B: I think I might need a new prescription for my eyeglasses.
options.C: My father also used to suffer from frequent migraines when he was younger.
options.D: I usually take some over-the-counter painkillers to manage the pain.
options.E: I have been feeling much better since I started exercising regularly.

questions.question: Boşluk 24 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Those features might increase the initial construction budget considerably.
options.B: I don't think the client is interested in environmental sustainability.
options.C: Solar panels are not very effective in this particular geographic location.
options.D: We should also consider using recycled materials for the interior design.
options.E: The aesthetic appeal of the building is more important than its efficiency.

questions.question: Boşluk 25 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: In contrast, a circular economy focuses on reusing, repairing, and recycling materials.
options.B: Many companies are still hesitant to adopt circular business models due to costs.
options.C: The linear economy has been the dominant model since the industrial revolution.
options.D: Governments are providing incentives for businesses to reduce their waste production.
options.E: Plastic waste is one of the most significant challenges for modern societies.

questions.question: Boşluk 26 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Research shows that children who are not exposed to language early struggle later.
options.B: Different languages have different grammatical structures and phonetic systems.
options.C: Bilingualism has been shown to have several cognitive benefits for young children.
options.D: The brain's plasticity allows adults to learn new languages, though with more effort.
options.E: Social interaction plays a minor role in the development of vocabulary in infants.

questions.question: Renewable energy sources, such as solar and wind power, are essential for reducing global carbon emissions. However, their intermittent nature poses a challenge for grid stability. _____. This would allow excess energy generated during sunny or windy periods to be used when demand is high.

options.A: Therefore, developing efficient energy storage systems is a top priority.
options.B: Solar panels are becoming more affordable for residential use worldwide.
options.C: Wind turbines can be installed both onshore and offshore to maximize output.
options.D: Nuclear energy is another low-carbon option that provides a steady power supply.
options.E: The transition to a green economy requires significant financial investment.

questions.question: Psychological resilience refers to an individual's ability to adapt to adversity and bounce back from difficult experiences. It is not a fixed trait but a dynamic process that can be developed over time. _____. These include having a strong support system, maintaining a positive outlook, and developing problem-solving skills.

options.A: Several factors contribute to the cultivation of resilience in individuals.
options.B: Resilience is often tested during times of significant life changes or trauma.
options.C: Many psychologists believe that genetics play a role in determining resilience.
options.D: Children who grow up in stable environments tend to be more resilient as adults.
options.E: The study of resilience has become increasingly popular in positive psychology.

questions.question: The experiment had to be called off because the laboratory equipment was malfunctioning.

options.A: The experiment was cancelled due to the failure of the lab tools.
options.B: The lab equipment worked well, but the experiment was still called off.
options.C: They decided to continue the experiment despite the equipment issues.
options.D: The equipment malfunctioned after the experiment had already finished.
options.E: They postponed the experiment until the equipment could be repaired.

questions.question: Hardly any students passed the exam, which indicates its extreme difficulty.

options.A: The fact that almost no students passed shows how hard the exam was.
options.B: Many students failed the exam because they did not study enough.
options.C: The exam was so easy that almost every student passed it easily.
options.D: The difficulty of the exam was expected by most of the students.
options.E: Only a few students found the exam to be difficult enough to fail.

questions.question: It is high time we took action to address the growing issue of plastic pollution.

options.A: We should have already started taking steps to solve the plastic problem.
options.B: There is still plenty of time to deal with the issue of plastic pollution.
options.C: Plastic pollution is a problem that will resolve itself over time.
options.D: We need to wait for more data before we act against plastic pollution.
options.E: The issue of plastic pollution is not as urgent as it used to be.

questions.question: (I) Dark matter is a mysterious substance that makes up about 27% of the universe. (II) It does not emit, absorb, or reflect light, making it invisible to traditional telescopes. (III) Astronomers have found many new planets in distant solar systems recently. (IV) Its existence is inferred from its gravitational effects on visible matter. (V) Understanding dark matter is one of the biggest challenges in modern astrophysics.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) The Renaissance was a period of great cultural and artistic flowering in Europe. (II) It began in Italy in the 14th century and later spread to the rest of the continent. (III) During this time, there was a renewed interest in the classical knowledge of Greece and Rome. (IV) The industrial revolution later changed the way goods were produced. (V) Famous artists like Leonardo da Vinci and Michelangelo emerged during this era.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) Artificial intelligence is transforming the healthcare industry in numerous ways. (II) AI algorithms can analyze medical images with high accuracy to detect diseases. (III) Many people are concerned about the privacy of their personal data online. (IV) It also helps in drug discovery by predicting how different compounds will interact. (V) Virtual assistants are being used to monitor patients' health and provide reminders.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, transformed the world from an agrarian society to an industrial one. It was characterized by the introduction of machinery, the growth of factories, and the development of new transportation systems. While it led to significant economic growth and technological advancement, it also brought about social challenges, such as poor working conditions and urban overcrowding. Question: According to the passage, what was one of the negative consequences of the Industrial Revolution?

options.A: The transition to an industrial society
options.B: The growth of the factory system
options.C: Improved transportation networks
options.D: Urban overcrowding and poor working conditions
options.E: Significant economic growth and development

questions.question: Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, transformed the world from an agrarian society to an industrial one. It was characterized by the introduction of machinery, the growth of factories, and the development of new transportation systems. While it led to significant economic growth and technological advancement, it also brought about social challenges, such as poor working conditions and urban overcrowding. Question: Where did the Industrial Revolution originate?

options.A: France
options.B: Germany
options.C: The United States
options.D: Britain
options.E: Italy

questions.question: Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, transformed the world from an agrarian society to an industrial one. It was characterized by the introduction of machinery, the growth of factories, and the development of new transportation systems. While it led to significant economic growth and technological advancement, it also brought about social challenges, such as poor working conditions and urban overcrowding. Question: What can be inferred about the world before the Industrial Revolution?

options.A: It was primarily an agrarian society.
options.B: It was already highly industrialized.
options.C: There were no social challenges at all.
options.D: Transportation systems were highly advanced.
options.E: Machinery was used extensively in all sectors.

questions.question: Passage: Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants and some other organisms use sunlight to synthesize foods with the help of chlorophyll. This process involves the intake of carbon dioxide and water and the release of oxygen as a byproduct. Photosynthesis is crucial for life on Earth as it provides the primary source of energy for nearly all food chains and maintains the level of oxygen in the atmosphere. Question: What is the primary role of chlorophyll in photosynthesis according to the text?

options.A: To release oxygen into the atmosphere
options.B: To absorb carbon dioxide from the air
options.C: To help synthesize food using sunlight
options.D: To convert water into energy directly
options.E: To protect the plant from excessive heat

questions.question: Passage: Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants and some other organisms use sunlight to synthesize foods with the help of chlorophyll. This process involves the intake of carbon dioxide and water and the release of oxygen as a byproduct. Photosynthesis is crucial for life on Earth as it provides the primary source of energy for nearly all food chains and maintains the level of oxygen in the atmosphere. Question: Which of the following is a byproduct of photosynthesis?

options.A: Carbon dioxide
options.B: Water
options.C: Sunlight
options.D: Oxygen
options.E: Chlorophyll

questions.question: Passage: Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants and some other organisms use sunlight to synthesize foods with the help of chlorophyll. This process involves the intake of carbon dioxide and water and the release of oxygen as a byproduct. Photosynthesis is crucial for life on Earth as it provides the primary source of energy for nearly all food chains and maintains the level of oxygen in the atmosphere. Question: Why is photosynthesis considered vital for life on Earth?

options.A: It is the only way plants can reproduce.
options.B: It helps in the formation of fossil fuels.
options.C: It provides energy for food chains and oxygen.
options.D: It prevents the occurrence of global warming.
options.E: It reduces the amount of water in the atmosphere.
